currency.name: Dominican peso (DOP)
currency.note: Relatively stable currency. Subject to tourism and remittances. USD widely accepted in tourist areas.

crypto.note: Crypto-friendly with growing adoption. Clear regulations developing. Active blockchain community.

article:
🧭 **Overview**
The Dominican Republic is Caribbean nation (shares Hispaniola island with Haiti) known for all-inclusive beach resorts (Punta Cana, Puerto Plata), baseball obsession (produces MLB stars), merengue/bachata music, and tourism boom. Santo Domingo is capital (oldest European settlement in Americas, Colonial Zone is UNESCO). The country offers affordable Caribbean lifestyle, beautiful beaches, vibrant culture, and growing economy. However, inequality, corruption, infrastructure gaps (power cuts), crime, and contrast with Haiti create challenges. Economy relies on tourism (25% of GDP), remittances, agriculture (sugar, tobacco, cocoa), and manufacturing.

👥 **People & vibe**
With roughly 11 million people, the Dominican Republic is ethnically mixed: mestizo/mulatto (~73%), white (~16%), Black (~11%). Spanish is language. Catholicism (~47%), Protestantism (~21%), no religion (~28%). The culture emphasizes baseball (national passion — produces Pujols, Ortiz, Sosa, hundreds of MLB players), merengue/bachata music and dance, family, and Caribbean laid-back pace. Dominicans are warm, welcoming, party-loving. The vibe is tropical beach meets Latin energy. Punta Cana is resort tourism; Santo Domingo is urban colonial charm; Santiago is second city; rural areas are simpler.

[[MAP]]

🌦️ **Climate & landscape**
Expect tropical climate: hot, humid year-round (25-31°C), rainy season (May-Nov), hurricane season (June-Nov — occasional devastating storms). The landscape includes Caribbean beaches (Punta Cana white sand, Samaná whale watching, Puerto Plata north coast), Pico Duarte (Caribbean's highest peak, 3,098m), rivers, waterfalls, and Zona Colonial (UNESCO). Natural beauty is diverse. Air quality is generally good except Santo Domingo (traffic pollution).

🏠 **Housing & settling in**
Punta Cana, Puerto Plata, Sosúa/Cabarete (north coast), Santo Domingo (Piantini, Naco, Zona Colonial) attract expats. Expect 1-2 months deposit. Rents: $400-1,500/month depending on area. Quality varies — gated communities (good security, amenities), older buildings basic. Power cuts are frequent (apagones — scheduled blackouts, infrastructure fails). Outside tourist areas and capital, infrastructure is limited. Security concerns exist (petty crime, some violent crime). Registration required.

💼 **Work & economy**
The economy is tourism (25% of GDP — all-inclusive resorts, 7M+ visitors annually), remittances (8% of GDP from 1.5M+ Dominicans in US), agriculture (sugar, tobacco, cocoa — historically important, declining), manufacturing (free zones, textiles), and mining (gold, silver). For foreigners, opportunities exist in tourism (resort management, diving/kitesurfing instruction), teaching English, real estate, call centers, or remote work. Work permits require employer sponsorship. Salaries are low ($600-2,000/month) but costs are low. Many work on tourist visas (technically illegal but common). Starting a business is feasible but bureaucracy and corruption are obstacles.

🛂 **Visa & entry**
Most nationalities get visa-free entry (30 days, tourist card $10 on arrival). For longer stays, residence permits (pensionado — $1,500/month income, rentista — $2,000/month, real estate purchase $200k+) exist. The process is bureaucratic but achievable. Permanent residence after 2 years temporary residence. Citizenship requires 2 years legal residence, Spanish proficiency, and renouncing other citizenship (rarely granted to foreigners).

🏥 **Healthcare**
Healthcare is two-tier. Public hospitals are overcrowded and poor quality. Private hospitals/clinics (CEDIMAT, Centro Médico UCE in Santo Domingo, Centro Médico Bournigal in Puerto Plata) offer good care at affordable prices (much cheaper than US). Medical tourism attracts Americans (plastic surgery, dental). Life expectancy is ~74 years. Dengue, Zika, chikungunya are risks. International health insurance supplements. Serious conditions may require travel to US.

🚗 **Transport & mobility**
Santo Domingo has Metro (2 lines, modern), buses, taxis, Uber. Most expats drive or use taxis/Uber. Roads vary — highways decent, secondary roads poor. Traffic is chaotic (aggressive driving, motorcycles everywhere, few rules). Driving is left-of-center chaos. Intercity buses (Caribe Tours, Metro) connect cities. The country is moderate size. Las Américas Airport (Santo Domingo), Punta Cana Airport connect internationally. Domestic flights to Puerto Plata, Samaná.

🍛 **Food note (national dish)**
The national dish is **La Bandera**: white rice, red beans, meat (chicken, beef, pork), salad, fried plantains. Served daily. Simple, filling. Alternatively, **Sancocho** (hearty stew) or **Mangu** (mashed plantains, breakfast). Dominican cuisine is Caribbean-Latin — rice, beans, plantains, yuca, influenced by Spanish, African, Taíno indigenous traditions.

🔎 **Bottom line**
The Dominican Republic suits beach lovers, retirees (affordable), digital nomads, baseball fans, kitesurfers/divers, and those seeking affordable Caribbean. Pros: beautiful beaches (Punta Cana, Samaná), affordability (cheaper than most Caribbean), warm people, vibrant music/dance culture, growing economy, and accessibility (close to US/Canada). Cons: inequality (resort luxury vs local poverty), power cuts (apagones — infrastructure fails, scheduled blackouts), crime (petty theft common, some violent crime), corruption, traffic chaos, and Haiti border tensions. Punta Cana is resort bubble; Santo Domingo is urban colonial; north coast is expat communities. Best for those with remote income, pensions, tourism jobs, or businesses catering to expats. The contrast with Haiti (shares island) is stark — DR is relatively prosperous, Haiti is collapsed. If you want affordable Caribbean beaches, baseball, and bachata and can handle power cuts and crime, DR delivers.

Expat Score — 7.0 / 10

national_dish.name: La Bandera / Sancocho / Mangu
national_dish.note: Rice-beans-meat or stew or mashed plantains — Caribbean-Latin staple. Rice, beans, plantains, yuca. Spanish/African/Taíno influenced.

lgbt_acceptance.note: Homosexuality is legal but social attitudes are conservative. No same-sex marriage or strong protections. Discrimination common. Santo Domingo and tourist areas more tolerant. Improving slowly.

personality.name: Juan Luis Guerra
personality.story: Singer-songwriter (merengue, bachata) who brought Dominican music to global stage. Grammy/Latin Grammy winner. Songs like 'Ojalá Que Llueva Café', 'Bachata Rosa' are classics. Represents Dominican cultural export and musical identity.

cards.title: ⚾ Baseball = national obsession
cards.text: Dominican Republic produces more MLB players per capita than anywhere (Albert Pujols, David Ortiz, Sammy Sosa, Pedro Martinez, hundreds more). Baseball academies everywhere. Kids play hoping for MLB contract (way out of poverty). Winter league (Lidom) is passionate. Baseball is religion — more important than politics.

cards.title: 💃 Merengue & Bachata = music identity
cards.text: Merengue (fast, energetic dance music) and bachata (romantic guitar music) originated here. Juan Luis Guerra brought global fame. Dancing is social glue — everyone dances. Clubs, streets, everywhere. Music is Dominican soul. UNESCO recognized bachata (2019).

cards.title: ⚡ Apagones = power cuts daily
cards.text: Power cuts (apagones) are scheduled daily (3-12 hours depending on area). Infrastructure can't meet demand. Generators/inverters are essential. Tourism zones get priority. It's frustrating reality — air conditioning cuts in 30°C heat. Electricity is unreliable despite infrastructure investment.

cards.title: 🏝️ Punta Cana = all-inclusive capital
cards.text: Punta Cana is all-inclusive resort mega-destination (white sand beaches, turquoise water). 3M+ visitors annually. Resorts are bubbles — tourists rarely see real DR. It's tourism engine but creates inequality (resort luxury vs local poverty). Economic impact is huge but benefits don't spread evenly.

cards.title: 🇭🇹 Haiti border = stark contrast
cards.text: DR-Haiti border shows world's sharpest inequality (satellite images show green DR, brown Haiti — deforestation). Anti-Haitian discrimination exists (Haitian migrants face racism, deportations). History is complicated (1937 Parsley Massacre killed 10,000+ Haitians). Border tensions persist. Sharing island creates uncomfortable juxtaposition.

cards.title: 🏛️ Colonial Zone = oldest Americas
cards.text: Santo Domingo's Zona Colonial (UNESCO) has oldest European settlement structures in Americas (1498+). Alcázar de Colón (Columbus family palace), Catedral Primada (first cathedral), Fortaleza Ozama. It's New World history birthplace. Tourism draw, charming cobblestones, but poverty exists blocks away.
